summary.brief: Clark v. Grant Med. Ctr.. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: Catherine, who was 62 years old by the time of trial and suffered from rheumatoid arthritis, intended to undergo hip replacement surgery on October 29, 2010.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Tierney made the first attempt to intubate Catherine in order to administer anesthesia but was unsuccessful.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Blair then made several attempts of his own using a laryngoscope but was not able to successfully intubate Catherine either.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Catherine underwent a CAT scan that revealed a perforation of the right pyriform sinus, which is very near the entrance to the esophagus.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Connell testified via deposition in his capacity both as a physician who participated in Catherine's care and as an expert witness.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Connell

evidence_index.text: Dr. Schrenzel said the fact that Catherine sustained a perforation is proof of excessive force, and if Dr. Blair had used the appropriate amount of force, 'a tear will not occur.'
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Schrenzel

evidence_index.text: Dr. Schrenzel also reviewed the consent form for anesthesia that Catherine signed prior to her planned hip replacement surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Schrenzel

evidence_index.text: Catherine testified that she has had rheumatoid arthritis since approximately 1975 and first started using a wheelchair in 2010.
evidence_index.speaker: Catherine Clark

evidence_index.text: Following deliberations, the jury returned a verdict in favor of the Clarks, awarding them $500,000 in damages.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court instructed the jury that '[a] physician is negligent if the physician fails to meet the required standard of care.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court instruction

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient with rheumatoid arthritis, scheduled for hip replacement surgery. Complained of stiff neck and difficulty opening mouth during preoperative assessment.

focused_exam.airway: Limited jaw range of motion, difficulty opening mouth, stiff neck

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Preparation for anesthesia for hip replacement
initial_differential: Difficult airway due to rheumatoid arthritis

requestables.name: CAT scan
requestables.description: Imaging to assess throat pain and swallowing difficulty

reveal.result_summary: Perforation of the right pyriform sinus
reveal.decision_impact: Required emergency surgery to repair perforation

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in operating room for hip replacement surgery
decision_points.prompt: What intubation technique should be used given the patient's medical history?

options.clinical_reasoning: Recommended for patients with difficult airway due to rheumatoid arthritis
Attempted despite known difficult airway conditions

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed airway was manageable with direct laryngoscopy

expected_action_court.description: Awake fiber-optic intubation should have been performed
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated this was the appropriate method

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform awake fiber-optic intubation led to perforation
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Awake fiber-optic intubation would have minimized risk
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient sustained a perforation of the right pyriform sinus

key_findings: Breach of duty in excessive force during intubation
Causation established - perforation due to intubation attempts

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Gentle technique would have prevented perforation

factual_timeline.event: Preoperative assessment noting stiff neck and limited mouth opening
Patient experiences throat pain and difficulty swallowing
